Baked Eggs with Feta, Harissa Tomato Sauce
& Coriander
Baked eggs is one of my all-time favourite breakfast dishes. There is no joy greater than that of plunging in a piece of bread and pulling it out coated in unctuous egg yolk and whatever else goes with the eggs. Ilike my eggs spicy. Chilli provides a great way ofkick-starting your metabolism in the morning and harissa is a versatile condiment to have in the house for quick dishes. Chunks of wonderfully salty, creamy feta provide the perfect match for harissa. This versatile breakfast dish has been relied on for many wonderful evening meals in my home.
SERVES *
4 tbsp olive oil
5 garlic cloves, bashed and thinly sliced
3 large red onions, cut in half and sliced into 1em- (½in-) thick half moons
2 tsp turmeric
1 tsp ground coriander
I tsp ground cumin
½ tsp ground cinnamon
3 tbsp harissa
6 large tomatoes, roughly chopped
400g can chopped tomatoes
sea salt flakes
2 x 20g packets of coriander, leaves picked and roughly chopped 400g (14oz) feta cheese freshly ground black pepper 8 large free-range eggs
* To make the sauce, preheat a saucepan over a medium heat and drizzle in just enough oil to lightly coat the base of the pan. Sauté the garlie slivers and onion slices until they begin to soften and become translucent. Add the turmeric, ground coriander, cumin and cinnamon and stir well before adding the harissa paste. Put in the fresh and canned tomatoes, season generously with sea salt flakes and give everything a good stir, then reduce the temperature slightly and allow the sauce to cook for 15-20 minutes, stirring occasionally to prevent sticking.
* Preheat the oven to 180°C/fan 160°C/gas mark 4. Select a large baking dish (or 2 smaller dishes) and pour the spicy tomato sauce into it. Scatter the chopped coriander on to the sauce (but save a handful for garnishing the finished dish) and give the mixture a very gentle stir to loosely incorporate the chopped leaves into the sauce. Break off 2.5cm (lin) rustic chunks of the feta and dot them all around the dish, pushing some down under the sauce and leaving some on the surface. Make 8 evenly spaced craters in the surface and crack your eggs into them, season the dish well with black pepper and another sprinkling of sea salt flakes, then bake for 10-12 minutes, or until the whites of the eggs have turned opaque and are cooked through.
* Remove from the oven, sprinkle the last handful of chopped coriander leaves over the dish and serve with ortancend (see page 55) is ideal as you can

     2→
     3→## Failures
     4→
     5→### 1. `Baked Eggs with Feta, Harissa Tomato Sauce & Coriander` → `''`
     6→
     7→**What happened:** Nothing was returned.
     8→
     9→**Why:** The title is split across two lines:
    10→```
    11→Baked Eggs with Feta, Harissa Tomato Sauce    ← line 1
    12→& Coriander                                    ← line 2
    13→```
    14→
    15→The 2-line join (`Baked Eggs with Feta, Harissa Tomato Sauce & Coriander`, 54 chars) passes hard filters. But the deduplication step removes it: the shorter single line `Baked Eggs with Feta, Harissa Tomato Sauce` is a substring of the join, so the join is discarded in favor of the partial line. After dedup only the partial survives — but it may not pass the threshold (no ALL_CAPS bonus, no structural bonus, mixed-case title).
    16→
    17→The deeper issue is that `& Coriander` is also a candidate (passes hard filters), and the dedup logic's "shorter wins" rule is designed to prevent ingredient run-ons but here it actively destroys the correct multi-line title join.
    18→
    19→If the partial single line does score above threshold, the output would be `Baked Eggs with Feta, Harissa Tomato Sauce` — not an exact match to expected — but the run reports `''`, suggesting either the partial also fails threshold, or something else causes all candidates to be filtered.
    20→
    21→**Root cause:** Split mixed-case title + deduplication's "shorter wins" rule destroys the 2-line join that is the correct answer.
    22→
    23→---
    24→
    25→### 2. `FINNISH MILK FLATBREADS + FINNISH POTATO FLATBREADS` → `FINNISH MILK FLATBREADS`
    26→
    27→**What happened:** Only the first title was returned; the second was dropped.
    28→
    29→**Why:** Both titles appear as clean standalone ALL_CAPS lines well within the document (lines 16 and 29 of 43). The algorithm should recognize both as a multi-recipe page.
    30→
    31→Tracing through:
    32→- `baseHeading` = `FINNISH MILK FLATBREADS` (first structural heading, passes `isStructuralHeading` — all three sig words ≥ 4 letters).
    33→- `firstStructuralHeading` = `FINNISH MILK FLATBREADS` (no continuation extension found — `FINNISH POTATO FLATBREADS` doesn't start with `"finnish milk flatbreads "`).
    34→- `FINNISH MILK FLATBREADS` receives `allCapsBonus (0.08) + structuralBonus (0.10) = 0.18` in total on top of its raw embedding score.
    35→
    36→The structural bonus inflates `bestBaseScore`, which raises the threshold via `bestBaseScore × 0.7`. `FINNISH POTATO FLATBREADS` has only `allCapsBonus (0.08)` and receives no position boost (it appears at ~65% of the document, past the 50% cutoff). Its total score falls below the inflated threshold.
    37→
    38→The multi-title guard (`allCapsSelected.length >= 2 → keep both`) never fires because `FINNISH POTATO FLATBREADS` is already filtered out before that point.
    39→
    40→**Root cause:** Structural heading bonus on the first title inflates `bestBaseScore`, raising the threshold so high that the second title — with identical ALL_CAPS quality but no structural bonus — fails to pass it.
    41→
    42→---
    43→
    44→### 3. `SAFFRON WHEAT BUNS WITH QUARK : COTTAGE CHEESE (VARIATION D)` → `FRON WHEAT BUNS (VARIATION 1)`
    45→
    46→**What happened:** A completely wrong, garbled OCR artifact was returned instead of the real title.
    47→
    48→**Why:** The page contains an earlier OCR corruption near line 29:
    49→```
    50→FRON WHEAT BUNS (VARIATION 1)
    51→```
    52→This is a truncated rendering of `SAFFRON WHEAT BUNS` with a garbled variation number. Despite being a junk artifact, it passes `isStructuralHeading`:
    53→- All letters uppercase ✓
    54→- sig words: `FRON`(4), `WHEAT`(5), `BUNS`(4), `(VARIATION`(9) — all ≥ 4 letters ✓
    55→
    56→So `baseHeading = "FRON WHEAT BUNS (VARIATION 1)"` and it receives the full `structuralBonus (0.10) + allCapsBonus (0.08) = 0.18`.
    57→
    58→The correct title appears later (lines 54–55):
    59→```
    60→SAFFRON WHEAT BUNS WITH QUARK
    61→/ COTTAGE CHEESE (VARIATION D)
    62→```
    63→
    64→The continuation extension logic was designed for exactly this pattern, but it only fires when `baseHeading` IS the correct title's first line — checking whether any candidate starts with `baseHeading.text + " "` followed by a continuation char. Since `baseHeading` is the wrong garbled line, the continuation logic never triggers for the real title.
    65→
    66→The 2-line join `SAFFRON WHEAT BUNS WITH QUARK / COTTAGE CHEESE (VARIATION D)` passes hard filters and would be the correct answer. But:
    67→1. The garbled `FRON WHEAT BUNS (VARIATION 1)` inflates `bestBaseScore` with its structural bonus, raising the threshold.
    68→2. The deduplication removes the join in favor of the shorter `SAFFRON WHEAT BUNS WITH QUARK` (which is a substring of the join).
    69→3. `SAFFRON WHEAT BUNS WITH QUARK` (no structural bonus, no position boost at 70% document depth) likely falls just below the inflated threshold.
    70→
    71→Result: only the garbled candidate survives.
    72→
    73→**Root cause:** A garbled ALL_CAPS OCR fragment passes `isStructuralHeading` and hijacks the structural heading role. This awards `firstStructuralHeading` to the wrong candidate, disables the continuation extension for the real title, and inflates the threshold so the real title is excluded.
    74→
    75→---
    76→
    77→## Common Themes
    78→
    79→### Theme 1: Structural bonus inflates threshold, excluding correct titles (cases 2 and 3)
    80→
    81→The comment at line 328 notes that `baseScore` (without position factor) is used for the threshold so that position boost doesn't inflate the bar. But `baseScore` still includes `allCapsBonus` and `structuralBonus`. The structural bonus (+0.10) is large enough that `bestBaseScore × 0.7` rises above what a correct ALL_CAPS title without the structural bonus can achieve. The fix attempted for position leakage was not applied to bonus leakage.
    82→
    83→### Theme 2: "Shorter wins" deduplication destroys multi-line title joins (cases 1 and 3)
    84→
    85→The dedup rule removes a candidate `a` if any shorter candidate `b` is a substring of it. This correctly prevents ingredient run-ons, but it also destroys 2-line title joins: the first half of the title (a shorter candidate) causes the complete join to be eliminated. The join is the only correct answer on split-title pages.
    86→
    87→### Theme 3: `isStructuralHeading` accepts garbled OCR fragments (case 3)
    88→
    89→The check requires ALL_CAPS, ≥2 sig words, and each sig word ≥4 uppercase letters. This is satisfied by truncated OCR artifacts like `FRON WHEAT BUNS (VARIATION 1)`. There is no check that the candidate is semantically plausible as a recipe title before awarding it the structural bonus and the `firstStructuralHeading` role.
    90→
    91→### Theme 4: Mixed-case titles receive no bonuses and may fall below the floor threshold (case 1)
    92→
    93→Titles like `Baked Eggs with Feta, Harissa Tomato Sauce & Coriander` get no `allCapsBonus` and no `structuralBonus`. With a floor threshold of `0.08`, a plausible recipe title that scores moderately in embeddings may still be filtered out — especially if any ALL_CAPS candidate (even a bad one) drives up `bestBaseScore`.

```
Baked Eggs with Feta, Harissa Tomato Sauce    ← line 1
& Coriander                                    ← line 2
```

The 2-line join (`Baked Eggs with Feta, Harissa Tomato Sauce & Coriander`, 54 chars) passes hard filters. But the deduplication step removes it: the shorter single line `Baked Eggs with Feta, Harissa Tomato Sauce` is a substring of the join, so the join is discarded in favor of the partial line. After dedup only the partial survives — but it may not pass the threshold (no ALL_CAPS bonus, no structural bonus, mixed-case title).

The deeper issue is that `& Coriander` is also a candidate (passes hard filters), and the dedup logic's "shorter wins" rule is designed to prevent ingredient run-ons but here it actively destroys the correct multi-line title join.

If the partial single line does score above threshold, the output would be `Baked Eggs with Feta, Harissa Tomato Sauce` — not an exact match to expected — but the run reports `''`, suggesting either the partial also fails threshold, or something else causes all candidates to be filtered.

**Root cause:** Split mixed-case title + deduplication's "shorter wins" rule destroys the 2-line join that is the correct answer.

---

### 2. `FINNISH MILK FLATBREADS + FINNISH POTATO FLATBREADS` → `FINNISH MILK FLATBREADS`

**What happened:** Only the first title was returned; the second was dropped.

**Why:** Both titles appear as clean standalone ALL_CAPS lines well within the document (lines 16 and 29 of 43). The algorithm should recognize both as a multi-recipe page.

Tracing through:
- `baseHeading` = `FINNISH MILK FLATBREADS` (first structural heading, passes `isStructuralHeading` — all three sig words ≥ 4 letters).
- `firstStructuralHeading` = `FINNISH MILK FLATBREADS` (no continuation extension found — `FINNISH POTATO FLATBREADS` doesn't start with `"finnish milk flatbreads "`).
- `FINNISH MILK FLATBREADS` receives `allCapsBonus (0.08) + structuralBonus (0.10) = 0.18` in total on top of its raw embedding score.

The structural bonus inflates `bestBaseScore`, which raises the threshold via `bestBaseScore × 0.7`. `FINNISH POTATO FLATBREADS` has only `allCapsBonus (0.08)` and receives no position boost (it appears at ~65% of the document, past the 50% cutoff). Its total score falls below the inflated threshold.

The multi-title guard (`allCapsSelected.length >= 2 → keep both`) never fires because `FINNISH POTATO FLATBREADS` is already filtered out before that point.

**Root cause:** Structural heading bonus on the first title inflates `bestBaseScore`, raising the threshold so high that the second title — with identical ALL_CAPS quality but no structural bonus — fails to pass it.

---

### 3. `SAFFRON WHEAT BUNS WITH QUARK : COTTAGE CHEESE (VARIATION D)` → `FRON WHEAT BUNS (VARIATION 1)`

**What happened:** A completely wrong, garbled OCR artifact was returned instead of the real title.

**Why:** The page contains an earlier OCR corruption near line 29:
```
FRON WHEAT BUNS (VARIATION 1)
```
This is a truncated rendering of `SAFFRON WHEAT BUNS` with a garbled variation number. Despite being a junk artifact, it passes `isStructuralHeading`:
- All letters uppercase ✓
- sig words: `FRON`(4), `WHEAT`(5), `BUNS`(4), `(VARIATION`(9) — all ≥ 4 letters ✓

So `baseHeading = "FRON WHEAT BUNS (VARIATION 1)"` and it receives the full `structuralBonus (0.10) + allCapsBonus (0.08) = 0.18`.

The correct title appears later (lines 54–55):
```
SAFFRON WHEAT BUNS WITH QUARK
/ COTTAGE CHEESE (VARIATION D)
```

The continuation extension logic was designed for exactly this pattern, but it only fires when `baseHeading` IS the correct title's first line — checking whether any candidate starts with `baseHeading.text + " "` followed by a continuation char. Since `baseHeading` is the wrong garbled line, the continuation logic never triggers for the real title.

The 2-line join `SAFFRON WHEAT BUNS WITH QUARK / COTTAGE CHEESE (VARIATION D)` passes hard filters and would be the correct answer. But:
1. The garbled `FRON WHEAT BUNS (VARIATION 1)` inflates `bestBaseScore` with its structural bonus, raising the threshold.
2. The deduplication removes the join in favor of the shorter `SAFFRON WHEAT BUNS WITH QUARK` (which is a substring of the join).
3. `SAFFRON WHEAT BUNS WITH QUARK` (no structural bonus, no position boost at 70% document depth) likely falls just below the inflated threshold.

Result: only the garbled candidate survives.

**Root cause:** A garbled ALL_CAPS OCR fragment passes `isStructuralHeading` and hijacks the structural heading role. This awards `firstStructuralHeading` to the wrong candidate, disables the continuation extension for the real title, and inflates the threshold so the real title is excluded.

---

## Common Themes

### Theme 1: Structural bonus inflates threshold, excluding correct titles (cases 2 and 3)

The comment at line 328 notes that `baseScore` (without position factor) is used for the threshold so that position boost doesn't inflate the bar. But `baseScore` still includes `allCapsBonus` and `structuralBonus`. The structural bonus (+0.10) is large enough that `bestBaseScore × 0.7` rises above what a correct ALL_CAPS title without the structural bonus can achieve. The fix attempted for position leakage was not applied to bonus leakage.

### Theme 2: "Shorter wins" deduplication destroys multi-line title joins (cases 1 and 3)

The dedup rule removes a candidate `a` if any shorter candidate `b` is a substring of it. This correctly prevents ingredient run-ons, but it also destroys 2-line title joins: the first half of the title (a shorter candidate) causes the complete join to be eliminated. The join is the only correct answer on split-title pages.

### Theme 3: `isStructuralHeading` accepts garbled OCR fragments (case 3)

The check requires ALL_CAPS, ≥2 sig words, and each sig word ≥4 uppercase letters. This is satisfied by truncated OCR artifacts like `FRON WHEAT BUNS (VARIATION 1)`. There is no check that the candidate is semantically plausible as a recipe title before awarding it the structural bonus and the `firstStructuralHeading` role.

### Theme 4: Mixed-case titles receive no bonuses and may fall below the floor threshold (case 1)

Titles like `Baked Eggs with Feta, Harissa Tomato Sauce & Coriander` get no `allCapsBonus` and no `structuralBonus`. With a floor threshold of `0.08`, a plausible recipe title that scores moderately in embeddings may still be filtered out — especially if any ALL_CAPS candidate (even a bad one) drives up `bestBaseScore`.

1→# Improvement Plan — Iteration 6
     2→
     3→## Why Iteration 5 Failed
     4→
     5→The iteration 4 plan correctly diagnosed all three failures and proposed five changes, but the critical ones (origin tracking, continuation join protection, embedding-based baseHeading, distributed structural bonus) were not implemented. The code still has:
     6→- No `origin` field on candidates — continuation join protection can't distinguish joins from singles
     7→- `baseHeading = candidates.find(isStructuralHeading)` — still purely positional
     8→- `structuralBonus` still exclusive to `firstStructuralHeading` (+0.10, one candidate only)
     9→
    10→This iteration re-proposes the essential changes with tighter implementation specifications.
    11→
    12→---
    13→
    14→## Root Cause Analysis
    15→
    16→### Failure 1: `Baked Eggs with Feta, Harissa Tomato Sauce & Coriander` → `''`
    17→
    18→The title is split across two OCR lines. The 2-line join passes hard filters but dedup removes it: the single-line fragment `Baked Eggs with Feta, Harissa Tomato Sauce` is a substring and shorter, so the join is discarded. The surviving partial likely falls below threshold (no ALL_CAPS bonus, no structural bonus).
    19→
    20→**Root cause:** Dedup's "shorter wins" rule cannot distinguish "title + ingredient run-on" (where shorter is correct) from "first half of split title + complete title join" (where longer is correct). The `& Coriander` continuation is the discriminating signal.
    21→
    22→### Failure 2: `FINNISH MILK FLATBREADS + FINNISH POTATO FLATBREADS` → `FINNISH MILK FLATBREADS`
    23→
    24→Both titles pass `isStructuralHeading`. But only the first gets `structuralBonus` (+0.10). This inflates `bestBaseScore`, which raises `threshold = bestBaseScore × 0.7`. The second title has only `allCapsBonus` (+0.08) and no position boost (at ~65% depth), so it falls below the inflated threshold. The multi-title guard never fires because the second title is already filtered out.
    25→
    26→**Root cause:** `structuralBonus` leaks into `bestBaseScore`, raising the bar so high that equally-valid structural headings without the bonus can't pass. The iteration 3 fix for position-boost leakage was not extended to bonus leakage.
    27→
    28→### Failure 3: `SAFFRON WHEAT BUNS WITH QUARK : COTTAGE CHEESE (VARIATION D)` → `FRON WHEAT BUNS (VARIATION 1)`
    29→
    30→The garbled OCR fragment `FRON WHEAT BUNS (VARIATION 1)` appears earlier in the document and passes `isStructuralHeading` (all sig words ≥ 4 uppercase letters). Being the first positional match, it becomes `baseHeading`, gets `structuralBonus` (+0.10), and hijacks the continuation extension logic. The correct title's 2-line join is also killed by dedup (same mechanism as Failure 1).
    31→
    32→**Root cause:** `baseHeading` selection is purely positional with no quality signal. A garbled OCR fragment that happens to satisfy `isStructuralHeading` can claim the structural heading slot over the real title.
    33→
    34→---
    35→
    36→## Proposed Changes
    37→
    38→### Change 1: Track candidate origin in `buildCandidates`
    39→
    40→**What:** Add an `origin` field (`"single" | "2-line" | "3-line"`) to candidate objects. This is pure metadata — no behavioral change — but is required by Change 2.
    41→
    42→**Where:** `title-extractor.ts`, `buildCandidates` function (lines 163–231).
    43→
    44→**Before:**
    45→```ts
    46→function buildCandidates(
    47→  lines: Array<string>
    48→): Array<{ text: string; position: number }> {
    49→```
    50→
    51→**After:**
    52→```ts
    53→export type CandidateOrigin = "single" | "2-line" | "3-line";
    54→
    55→function buildCandidates(
    56→  lines: Array<string>
    57→): Array<{ text: string; position: number; origin: CandidateOrigin }> {
    58→```
    59→
    60→Tag each `candidates.push(...)` call:
    61→- Line 187: `candidates.push({ text: line.text, position: line.index, origin: "single" });`
    62→- Line 198: `candidates.push({ text: joined2, position: line.index, origin: "2-line" });`
    63→- Line 210: `candidates.push({ text: joined3, position: line.index, origin: "3-line" });`
    64→
    65→Propagate the `origin` field through `scored` and `selected` arrays (add to their type signatures).
    66→
    67→**Impact:** None by itself. Enables Change 2.
    68→
    69→---
    70→
    71→### Change 2: Protect continuation joins from dedup (fixes Failures 1 and 3-secondary)
    72→
    73→**What:** Before the dedup filter, remove single-line candidates that are a prefix of a surviving multi-line join whose continuation part begins with a continuation character (`/`, `&`, `+`, `:`, `(`). This prevents the "shorter wins" rule from destroying valid title joins.
    74→
    75→**Where:** New block inserted between the structural-heading prefix removal (line 352) and the dedup filter (line 359). The dedup block itself is NOT modified (respecting the guard comment).
    76→
    77→**Logic:**
    78→```ts
    79→// Protect continuation joins: when a multi-line join survived the threshold and
    80→// its second part starts with a continuation character, remove the single-line
    81→// prefix so dedup can't destroy the complete join.
    82→const survivingJoins = selected.filter(
    83→  (s) => s.origin === "2-line" || s.origin === "3-line"
    84→);
    85→if (survivingJoins.length > 0) {
    86→  selected = selected.filter((s) => {
    87→    if (s.origin !== "single") return true;
    88→    const sLower = s.text.toLowerCase();
    89→    return !survivingJoins.some((j) => {
    90→      const jLower = j.text.toLowerCase();
    91→      if (!jLower.startsWith(sLower + " ")) return false;
    92→      const remainder = jLower.slice(sLower.length + 1);
    93→      return /^[/&+:(]/.test(remainder);
    94→    });
    95→  });
    96→}
    97→```
    98→
    99→**Why safe for Pierogi Ruskie:** The join `"Pierogi Ruskie 200g mąki 3 ziemniaki"` has a continuation starting with `"200g"` — a digit, not a continuation character. The prefix `"Pierogi Ruskie"` is preserved, dedup correctly picks it. No behavioral change for non-continuation joins.
   100→
   101→**Expected impact:** Fixes Failure 1 — the join `"Baked Eggs with Feta, Harissa Tomato Sauce & Coriander"` is protected (continuation starts with `&`). Also fixes the secondary issue in Failure 3 — the join `"SAFFRON WHEAT BUNS WITH QUARK / COTTAGE CHEESE (VARIATION D)"` is protected (continuation starts with `/`).
   102→
   103→---
   104→
   105→### Change 3: Exclude `structuralBonus` from threshold computation (fixes Failure 2)
   106→
   107→**What:** Compute `bestBaseScore` using `rawScore + allCapsBonus` only, excluding `structuralBonus` (and `firstHeadingBonus` if Change 4 adds one). The structural bonus still helps the heading win the ranking, but does not inflate the threshold that other candidates must pass.
   108→
   109→**Where:** Scoring loop and threshold computation (lines 320–332).
   110→
   111→**Before:**
   112→```ts
   113→const baseScore = rawScore + allCapsBonus + structuralBonus;
   114→// ...
   115→const bestBaseScore = Math.max(...scored.map((s) => s.baseScore));
   116→const threshold = Math.max(0.08, bestBaseScore * 0.7);
   117→```
   118→
   119→**After:** Add a new field `thresholdScore` that excludes structural bonus:
   120→```ts
   121→const baseScore = rawScore + allCapsBonus + structuralBonus;
   122→const thresholdScore = rawScore + allCapsBonus; // no structural bonus
   123→// ...
   124→const bestThresholdScore = Math.max(...scored.map((s) => s.thresholdScore));
   125→const threshold = Math.max(0.08, bestThresholdScore * 0.7);
   126→```
   127→
   128→**Why this works:** On the Finnish Flatbreads page, `FINNISH MILK FLATBREADS` has `rawScore ≈ X`, `allCapsBonus = 0.08`, `structuralBonus = 0.10`. Previously `bestBaseScore = X + 0.18` and `threshold = (X + 0.18) × 0.7`. Now `bestThresholdScore = X + 0.08` and `threshold = (X + 0.08) × 0.7`. The second title with `score = X' + 0.08` (similar rawScore, same allCapsBonus) easily clears this lower bar.
   129→
   130→**Why safe:** The structural bonus still contributes to `score` and `baseScore` for ranking purposes — it just doesn't inflate the threshold. Single-recipe pages are unaffected because there's only one candidate to rank.
   131→
   132→**Expected impact:** Fixes Failure 2. Both Finnish titles pass the threshold; the multi-title guard (`allCapsSelected.length >= 2`) fires and returns both.
   133→
   134→---
   135→
   136→### Change 4: Select `baseHeading` by embedding quality, not position (fixes Failure 3-primary)
   137→
   138→**What:** Defer `baseHeading` selection until after embeddings are computed. Instead of `candidates.find(isStructuralHeading)` (first positional match), pick the structural heading candidate with the highest `rawScore`.
   139→
   140→**Where:** Move structural heading selection from lines 278–292 to after the scoring loop (after line 323).
   141→
   142→**Before (line 278):**
   143→```ts
   144→const baseHeading = candidates.find(isStructuralHeading);
   145→```
   146→
   147→**After (after scoring loop, before threshold computation):**
   148→```ts
   149→// Select baseHeading by embedding quality (rawScore), not position.
   150→// This prevents OCR-garbled fragments from claiming the structural heading slot.
   151→const structuralCandidateScores = scored.filter((s) => {
   152→  const orig = candidates.find((c) => c.text === s.text);
   153→  return orig && isStructuralHeading(orig);
   154→});
   155→const bestStructural = structuralCandidateScores.length > 0
   156→  ? structuralCandidateScores.reduce((a, b) => a.rawScore > b.rawScore ? a : b)
   157→  : null;
   158→const baseHeading = bestStructural
   159→  ? candidates.find((c) => c.text === bestStructural.text)
   160→  : undefined;
   161→```
   162→
   163→This requires restructuring the scoring loop slightly: compute `rawScore` first (without bonuses), then select `baseHeading`, then assign bonuses in a second pass. The restructuring adds no new embedding calls.
   164→
   165→**Restructured scoring flow:**
   166→```ts
   167→// Pass 1: compute rawScore for all candidates
   168→const rawScored = [];
   169→for (const candidate of candidates) {
   170→  const embedding = await embed(candidate.text);
   171→  const titleSim = cosineSimilarity(embedding, cachedTitleRefEmbedding);
   172→  const headerSim = cosineSimilarity(embedding, cachedHeaderRefEmbedding);
   173→  const noiseSim = cosineSimilarity(embedding, cachedNoiseRefEmbedding);
   174→  const rawScore = titleSim - Math.max(headerSim, noiseSim);
   175→  rawScored.push({ ...candidate, rawScore, embedding });
   176→}
   177→
   178→// Select baseHeading by rawScore quality
   179→const structuralCandidates = rawScored.filter((s) => isStructuralHeading(s));
   180→const bestStructural = structuralCandidates.length > 0
   181→  ? structuralCandidates.reduce((a, b) => a.rawScore > b.rawScore ? a : b)
   182→  : null;
   183→const baseHeading = bestStructural ?? undefined;
   184→
   185→// Derive firstStructuralHeading (continuation extension) — same logic as before
   186→const firstStructuralHeading = baseHeading && (
   187→  rawScored.find((c) => {
   188→    if (!isStructuralHeading(c)) return false;
   189→    const hLower = baseHeading.text.toLowerCase();
   190→    const cLower = c.text.toLowerCase();
   191→    return cLower.startsWith(hLower + " ") && /^[/&+:(]/.test(cLower.slice(hLower.length + 1));
   192→  }) ?? baseHeading
   193→);
   194→
   195→// Pass 2: apply bonuses
   196→const scored = rawScored.map((rs) => {
   197→  const relativePosition = rs.position / lines.length;
   198→  const positionFactor = relativePosition < 0.5 ? 1.0 + 0.12 * (1 - relativePosition * 2) : 1.0;
   199→  const allCapsBonus = isAllCaps(rs.text) && rs.text.replace(/[^a-zA-Z]/g, "").length >= 4 ? 0.08 : 0;
   200→  const structuralBonus = firstStructuralHeading && rs.text === firstStructuralHeading.text ? 0.10 : 0;
   201→  const thresholdScore = rs.rawScore + allCapsBonus;
   202→  const baseScore = rs.rawScore + allCapsBonus + structuralBonus;
   203→  const score = rs.rawScore * positionFactor + allCapsBonus + structuralBonus;
   204→  return { ...rs, score, rawScore: rs.rawScore, baseScore, thresholdScore };
   205→});
   206→```
   207→
   208→**Why this works:** `FRON WHEAT BUNS (VARIATION 1)` is garbled — its MiniLM embedding will have low similarity to the title reference ("recipe name, dish title") compared to `SAFFRON WHEAT BUNS WITH QUARK`, a plausible recipe title. The correct heading wins `baseHeading`, continuation extension fires for the `/` join, and the structural bonus goes to the right candidate.
   209→
   210→**Performance:** Zero additional embedding calls. Same candidates, same embeddings, just reordered logic.
   211→
   212→**Expected impact:** Fixes the primary bug in Failure 3.
   213→
   214→---
   215→
   216→### Change 5: OCR truncation cross-validation (defense-in-depth for Failure 3)
   217→
   218→**What:** After selecting `bestStructural` in Change 4, add a lightweight cross-validation: if a significant word in one structural heading is a strict suffix of a significant word in another structural heading, penalize the shorter-word candidate's rawScore by -0.15. This catches `FRON` being a suffix of `SAFFRON`.
   219→
   220→**Where:** Between structural candidate filtering and `bestStructural` selection in Change 4.
   221→
   222→**Logic:**
   223→```ts
   224→// Penalize structural headings with truncated OCR words:
   225→// if "FRON" appears and another heading has "SAFFRON", "FRON" is likely garbled
   226→for (const sc of structuralCandidates) {
   227→  const sigWords = sc.text.split(/\s+/).filter((w) => w.replace(/[^A-Z]/g, "").length >= 4);
   228→  for (const other of structuralCandidates) {
   229→    if (other === sc) continue;
   230→    const otherSigWords = other.text.split(/\s+/).filter((w) => w.replace(/[^A-Z]/g, "").length >= 4);
   231→    const hasTruncation = sigWords.some((w) =>
   232→      otherSigWords.some((ow) => ow !== w && ow.length > w.length && ow.endsWith(w))
   233→    );
   234→    if (hasTruncation) {
   235→      sc.rawScore -= 0.15;
   236→      break;
   237→    }
   238→  }
   239→}
   240→```
   241→
   242→**Why safe:** Only fires when two structural headings share a suffix relationship — extremely unlikely to produce false positives. On the Finnish page, `FLATBREADS` appears in both titles but `ow !== w` prevents self-matching and neither is a suffix of the other.
   243→
   244→**Expected impact:** Additional safety for Failure 3. Even if embeddings alone don't sufficiently separate the garbled candidate, the -0.15 penalty ensures it loses.
   245→
   246→---
   247→
   248→## Summary
   249→
   250→| Change | Fixes | Risk | Scope |
   251→|---|---|---|---|
   252→| 1. Track `origin` field | Enables #2 | None (metadata only) | `buildCandidates` return type |
   253→| 2. Protect continuation joins | F1, F3 secondary | Low — continuation char guard | New block before dedup |
   254→| 3. Exclude structural bonus from threshold | F2 | Low — bonus still helps ranking | Threshold computation (2 lines) |
   255→| 4. Embedding-quality baseHeading | F3 primary | Low — better signal, same data | Restructure scoring into 2 passes |
   256→| 5. OCR truncation penalty | F3 defense | Very low — strict cross-match only | New block in structural selection |
   257→
   258→### Performance
   259→
   260→- **Zero additional embedding calls.** All changes reorder existing logic or add O(25²) string comparisons.
   261→- **No new dependencies.** All changes within `title-extractor.ts`.
   262→- **Mobile-safe.** Well under the 10-second budget.
   263→
   264→### Expected Accuracy
   265→
   266→- **F1 (Baked Eggs):** Fixed by Changes 1+2. Join is protected from dedup → `"Baked Eggs with Feta, Harissa Tomato Sauce & Coriander"` survives.
   267→- **F2 (Finnish Flatbreads):** Fixed by Change 3. Threshold no longer inflated by structural bonus → both titles pass → multi-title guard joins them with `+`.
   268→- **F3 (Saffron Wheat Buns):** Fixed by Changes 4+5 (correct baseHeading) + Changes 1+2 (correct join protected) → `"SAFFRON WHEAT BUNS WITH QUARK / COTTAGE CHEESE (VARIATION D)"` is returned.
   269→
   270→### Regression Risks
   271→
   272→- **Pierogi Ruskie:** Change 2 does NOT protect this join (continuation starts with `"200g"`, a digit). Dedup correctly keeps the shorter form.
   273→- **ARAYES SHRAK:** Existing structural heading prefix removal (lines 337–352) still fires. Change 2 adds a parallel protection path but doesn't conflict.
   274→- **MIXED SEED CRISPBREAD / OVERNIGHT PIZZA DOUGH:** Single structural headings. Change 3 doesn't affect threshold when there's only one strong candidate. Change 4 picks the same heading (only one structural candidate).
   275→
   276→### Key Difference from Iteration 4 Plan

   400→    thread.start()
   401→
   402→    # Live display loop
   403→    stall_timeout = CLAUDE_STALL_TIMEOUT_BY_MODEL.get(model, CLAUDE_STALL_TIMEOUT)
   404→    stalled = False
   405→    try:
   406→        while not reader_done.is_set():
   407→            elapsed = time.time() - start
   408→
   409→            # Overall timeout check
   410→            if elapsed > CLAUDE_TIMEOUT:
   411→                proc.kill()
   412→                break
   413→
   414→            # Stall detection: no output for stall_timeout seconds
   415→            with lock:
   416→                silence = time.time() - last_activity
   417→            if silence > stall_timeout:
   418→                stalled = True
   419→                proc.kill()
   420→                break
   421→
   422→            _clear_display(display_height)
   423→            display_height = _render_display(model, elapsed, recent_lines)
   424→
   425→            reader_done.wait(timeout=1.0)
   426→
   427→        # Final display update
   428→        elapsed = time.time() - start
   429→        _clear_display(display_height)
   430→        display_height = _render_display(model, elapsed, recent_lines)
   431→
   432→    except KeyboardInterrupt:
   433→        proc.kill()
   434→        _clear_display(display_height)
   435→        print(f"  Claude ({model}) interrupted by user")
   436→        raise
   437→
   438→    # Wait for process to finish
   439→    try:
   440→        proc.wait(timeout=30)
   441→    except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
   442→        proc.kill()
   443→        proc.wait()
   444→
   445→    stderr_output = proc.stderr.read()
   446→    elapsed = time.time() - start
   447→
   448→    # Use result from stream; fall back to joining raw output
   449→    full_output = result_text or "".join(raw_lines)
   450→
   451→    # Save log (always, even on failure)
   452→    if log_path:
   453→        log_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   454→        with open(log_path, "w") as f:
   455→            f.write(f"# Claude ({model}) — {time.strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')}\n")
   456→            f.write(f"# Duration: {elapsed:.0f}s — Exit: {proc.returncode}\n")
   457→            f.write(f"# Prompt:\n")
   458→            for pline in prompt.splitlines():
   459→                f.write(f"#   {pline}\n")
   460→            f.write(f"\n{'=' * 60}\n\n")
   461→            f.write(result_text or "")
   462→            if stderr_output:
   463→                f.write(f"\n{'=' * 60}\n# STDERR:\n{stderr_output}\n")
   464→            # Also save raw NDJSON stream for debugging
   465→            f.write(f"\n{'=' * 60}\n# RAW STREAM ({len(raw_lines)} events):\n")
   466→            for rl in raw_lines:
   467→                f.write(rl)
   468→
   469→    # Status line and error handling
   470→    if stalled:
   471→        msg = f"Claude ({model}) stalled (no output for {stall_timeout}s) at {elapsed:.0f}s"
   472→        print(f"\n  {msg}")
   473→        raise ClaudeStallError(msg)
   474→    elif proc.returncode == 0:
   475→        print(f"\n  Claude ({model}) finished in {elapsed:.0f}s")
   476→    elif elapsed > CLAUDE_TIMEOUT:
   477→        print(f"\n  Claude ({model}) timed out after {elapsed:.0f}s")
   478→        raise RuntimeError(f"Claude ({model}) timed out after {elapsed:.0f}s")
   479→    else:
   480→        msg = f"Claude ({model}) failed (exit {proc.returncode}) in {elapsed:.0f}s"
   481→        print(f"\n  {msg}")
   482→        if stderr_output:
   483→            print(f"  stderr: {stderr_output[:500]}")
   484→        raise RuntimeError(f"{msg}\n{stderr_output[:1000]}")
   485→
   486→    return result_text or full_output
   487→
   488→
   489→def run_claude(
   490→    prompt: str,
   491→    model: str = "sonnet",
   492→    log_path: Path | None = None,
   493→) -> str:
   494→    """
   495→    Run Claude Code with automatic retry on stalls.
   496→
   497→    Retries up to CLAUDE_MAX_RETRIES times when Claude stalls (no output for
   498→    CLAUDE_STALL_TIMEOUT seconds). After exhausting retries, raises RuntimeError.
   499→    Non-stall failures (exit code, overall timeout) are raised immediately.
   500→    """
   501→    for attempt in range(1, CLAUDE_MAX_RETRIES + 1):
   502→        try:
   503→            return _run_claude_once(prompt, model=model, log_path=log_path)
   504→        except ClaudeStallError:
   505→            if attempt < CLAUDE_MAX_RETRIES:
   506→                print(f"  Retrying ({attempt}/{CLAUDE_MAX_RETRIES})...")
   507→            else:
   508→                raise RuntimeError(
   509→                    f"Claude ({model}) stalled {CLAUDE_MAX_RETRIES} times — giving up"
   510→                )
   511→    # unreachable, but satisfies type checker
   512→    raise RuntimeError("unreachable")
   513→
   514→
   515→# --- Iteration phases ---
   516→
   517→def phase_evaluate(iteration: int, log_dir: Path) -> tuple[Path, float, list[dict]]:
   518→    """Run evaluation, write results, log accuracy. Returns (iter_dir, accuracy, results)."""
   519→    print(f"\nEvaluating real input files...")
   520→    real_files = sorted(glob.glob(str(INPUT_DIR / "*.real.txt")))
   521→    print(f"  Found {len(real_files)} real files")
   522→    real_results = evaluate_files(real_files)
   523→    real_accuracy = compute_accuracy(real_results)
   524→    print(f"\n  Real accuracy: {real_accuracy:.1%} ({sum(1 for r in real_results if r['match'])}/{len(real_results)})")
   525→
   526→    gen_results: list[dict] = []
   527→    combined_accuracy = real_accuracy
   528→
   529→    # If real accuracy is high enough, test on generated data too
   530→    if real_accuracy >= ACCURACY_THRESHOLD:
   531→        print(f"\nGenerating {SYNTHETIC_COUNT} synthetic OCR files...")
   532→        generate_synthetic_data(iteration, log_dir=log_dir)
   533→
   534→        print(f"\nEvaluating generated files...")
   535→        gen_files = sorted(glob.glob(str(INPUT_DIR / f"*.generated.{iteration}.txt")))
   536→        print(f"  Found {len(gen_files)} generated files")
   537→        if gen_files:
   538→            gen_results = evaluate_files(gen_files)
   539→            gen_accuracy = compute_accuracy(gen_results)
   540→            print(f"\n  Generated accuracy: {gen_accuracy:.1%}")
   541→            combined_accuracy = compute_accuracy(real_results + gen_results)
   542→        else:
   543→            print("  WARNING: No generated files found. Claude may have failed.")
   544→
   545→    all_results = real_results + gen_results
   546→    print(f"\n  Combined accuracy: {combined_accuracy:.1%}")
   547→
   548→    # Log iteration accuracy
   549→    with open(ITER_LOG, "a") as f:
   550→        f.write(f"{iteration} - {combined_accuracy:.1%}\n")
   551→
   552→    # Create iteration directory and write results
   553→    commit_hash = get_commit_hash()
   554→    iter_dir = DOCS_DIR / f"{iteration}-{commit_hash}"
   555→    write_results(iter_dir, all_results)
   556→    print(f"  Results written to {iter_dir.relative_to(PROJECT_ROOT)}")
   557→
   558→    return iter_dir, combined_accuracy, all_results
   559→
   560→
   561→def generate_synthetic_data(iteration: int, log_dir: Path) -> None:
   562→    """Generate synthetic OCR data using Claude Code."""
   563→    real_files = glob.glob(str(INPUT_DIR / "*.real.txt"))
   564→    existing_titles = [extract_expected_title(f) for f in real_files]
   565→    titles_list = ", ".join(existing_titles)
   566→
   567→    prompt = f"""Generate {SYNTHETIC_COUNT} realistic fake OCR recipe text files.
   568→
   569→Each file should simulate real OCR output from a scanned cookbook page. Include typical
   570→OCR artifacts: slightly misrecognized characters, broken line wraps, page numbers,
   571→headers/footers, section labels.
   572→
   573→Save each file to: tools/title-loop/input/{{RECIPE_TITLE}}.generated.{iteration}.txt
   574→
   575→The recipe title in the filename must EXACTLY match the main title as it appears in the
   576→generated text content (case-insensitive matching is fine, but the words must match).
   577→
   578→Requirements:
   579→- Mix of English and Polish recipes (roughly 60/40)
   580→- Variety: soups, mains, desserts, salads, appetizers, breads, drinks, preserves
   581→- Include multi-word titles (2-5 words), some single-word titles
   582→- Include OCR noise: garbled short lines at top/bottom, stray numbers, broken words
   583→- Some files should have the title after noise/headers (not first line)
   584→- Vary title position: top, after page number, after section header, mid-page
   585→- Some should have section headers like INGREDIENTS, SKŁADNIKI before or near the title
   586→- Generate DIFFERENT recipes than these existing ones: {titles_list}
   587→- Each file should be 15-40 lines long
   588→
   589→Generate all {SYNTHETIC_COUNT} files. Work through them systematically."""
   590→
   591→    run_claude(prompt, model="haiku", log_path=log_dir / "generate.log")
   592→
   593→
   594→def phase_analyze(iter_dir: Path, results: list[dict]) -> None:
   595→    """Analyze extraction failures with Claude (Sonnet)."""
   596→    log_dir = iter_dir / "logs"
   597→    failures = [r for r in results if not r["match"]]
   598→    if not failures:
   599→        (iter_dir / "feedback.md").write_text("No failures to analyze — all extractions matched.\n")
   600→        return
   601→
   602→    failure_details = "\n".join(
   603→        f"- File: {r['file']}\n  Expected: '{r['expected']}'\n  Got: '{r['extracted']}'"
   604→        for r in failures
   605→    )
   606→
   607→    prompt = f"""Analyze the title extraction failures below. Read each failing input file
   608→and the current implementation at lib/text-classifier/title-extractor.ts.
   609→
   610→Write your analysis to {iter_dir.relative_to(PROJECT_ROOT)}/feedback.md explaining:
   611→1. What patterns the algorithm fails on
   612→2. Why each specific failure occurred
   613→3. Common themes across failures
   614→
   615→Do NOT modify any code files. Only write the feedback.md file.
   616→
   617→Failures:
   618→{failure_details}
   619→
   620→Results file: {iter_dir.relative_to(PROJECT_ROOT)}/results.txt"""
   621→
   622→    run_claude(prompt, model="sonnet", log_path=log_dir / "analyze.log")
   623→
   624→
   625→def phase_plan(iter_dir: Path, iteration: int) -> None:
   626→    """Plan algorithm improvements with Claude (Opus)."""
   627→    log_dir = iter_dir / "logs"
   628→    prompt = f"""Read the feedback at {iter_dir.relative_to(PROJECT_ROOT)}/feedback.md
   629→and the current title extraction implementation at lib/text-classifier/title-extractor.ts.
   630→Also read previous iteration docs in tools/title-loop/docs/ to understand what has been tried.
   631→
   632→Your job: propose changes that fix every failure. Think creatively and ambitiously.
   633→
   634→Hard constraints:
   635→- Must run on a mobile device (React Native / Expo)
   636→- Total title extraction must complete under 10 seconds
   637→- Changes land in lib/text-classifier/ and related files
   638→
   639→Everything else is open for debate. The current approach (MiniLM embeddings + heuristic scoring)
   640→is one option, but you are free to propose alternatives if they'd work better:
   641→- Different embedding model or similarity strategy
   642→- Replacing embeddings entirely with a rule-based or hybrid approach
   643→- Restructuring the pipeline (e.g., two-pass, re-ranking, candidate merging)
   644→- Changing how candidates are generated, filtered, or deduplicated
   645→- Any other approach that solves the failures within the hard constraints
   646→
   647→Don't be incremental for the sake of it — if the feedback reveals a fundamental design flaw,
   648→propose a fundamental fix. But also don't change things that already work well.
   649→
   650→Before writing any files, briefly outline your key proposed changes (2-3 sentences per failure)
   651→so you can think through the approach first.
   652→
   653→Then write a detailed improvement plan to {iter_dir.relative_to(PROJECT_ROOT)}/improvement-plan.md
   654→that includes:
   655→1. Root cause analysis for each failure pattern
   656→2. Specific code changes with before/after examples
   657→3. New or modified heuristics, scoring adjustments, or pipeline changes
   658→4. Expected impact on accuracy
   659→
   660→Do NOT modify any code files. Only write the improvement-plan.md file."""
   661→
   662→    run_claude(prompt, model="opus", log_path=log_dir / "plan.log")
   663→
   664→
   665→def phase_execute(iter_dir: Path) -> None:
   666→    """Execute improvements with Claude (Sonnet), verify, and commit."""
   667→    log_dir = iter_dir / "logs"
   668→    prompt = f"""Execute the improvement plan at {iter_dir.relative_to(PROJECT_ROOT)}/improvement-plan.md.
   669→
   670→Modify lib/text-classifier/title-extractor.ts and any related files as needed.
   671→Make sure:
   672→1. All existing tests still pass
   673→2. The CLI at tools/title-loop/extract-title.ts still works
   674→3. The code is clean and follows the project conventions in AGENTS.md
   675→4. No unnecessary changes outside the title extraction logic
   676→
   677→CRITICAL CONSTRAINT: Do NOT modify the substring deduplication logic in title-extractor.ts.
   678→The block starting with "// Deduplicate: if one title is a substring of another, keep the shorter"
   679→must remain exactly as-is. It keeps the SHORTER candidate. Do not invert this to keep the longer.
   680→This has been incorrectly changed 5 times and breaks tests every time.
   681→
   682→After making changes, run: npx vitest run --globals lib/text-classifier/__tests__/title-extractor.test.ts
   683→Fix any test failures before finishing."""
   684→
   685→    run_claude(prompt, model="sonnet", log_path=log_dir / "execute.log")
   686→
   687→    # Verify tests and CLI
   688→    print(f"\nVerifying tests and CLI...")
   689→    tests_ok = run_tests()
   690→    cli_ok = verify_cli()
   691→
   692→    if not tests_ok or not cli_ok:
   693→        print("  WARNING: Verification failed. Attempting to fix...")
   694→        fix_prompt = """The title extraction tests or CLI are failing after recent changes.
   695→Please fix any issues in lib/text-classifier/title-extractor.ts.
   696→Run: npx vitest run lib/text-classifier/__tests__/title-extractor.test.ts
   697→And verify: npx tsx tools/title-loop/extract-title.ts tools/title-loop/input/*.real.txt
   698→Make sure both pass before finishing."""
   699→        run_claude(fix_prompt, model="sonnet", log_path=log_dir / "fix.log")
   700→
   701→        if not run_tests() or not verify_cli():
   702→            raise RuntimeError("Tests or CLI still failing after fix attempt")
   703→
   704→
   705→def run_tests() -> bool:
   706→    """Run the title extraction tests."""
   707→    print("  Running tests...")
   708→    result = subprocess.run(
   709→        ["npx", "vitest", "run", "--globals", "lib/text-classifier/__tests__/title-extractor.test.ts"],
   710→        capture_output=True,
   711→        text=True,
   712→        timeout=120,
   713→        cwd=str(PROJECT_ROOT),
   714→    )
   715→    if result.returncode != 0:
   716→        print(f"  Tests FAILED: {result.stdout[-500:]}")
   717→        return False
   718→    print("  Tests passed.")
   719→    return True
   720→
   721→
   722→def verify_cli() -> bool:
   723→    """Verify the CLI script runs without errors on a sample file."""
   724→    real_files = glob.glob(str(INPUT_DIR / "*.real.txt"))
   725→    if not real_files:
   726→        print("  No real files to test CLI with")
   727→        return False
   728→
   729→    sample = real_files[0]
   730→    print(f"  Verifying CLI on: {Path(sample).name}")
   731→    result = subprocess.run(
   732→        ["npx", "tsx", str(EXTRACT_SCRIPT), sample],
   733→        capture_output=True,
   734→        text=True,
   735→        timeout=EXTRACT_TIMEOUT,
   736→        cwd=str(PROJECT_ROOT),
   737→    )
   738→    if result.returncode != 0:
   739→        print(f"  CLI verification FAILED: {result.stderr[:300]}")
   740→        return False
   741→    print(f"  CLI output: '{result.stdout.strip()}'")
   742→    return True
   743→
   744→
   745→# --- Main loop ---
   746→
   747→def main() -> None:
   748→    """Main self-improving loop with full mid-iteration resumability."""
   749→    DOCS_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   750→
   751→    last_logged = get_last_logged_iteration()
   752→    if last_logged > 0:
   753→        last_phase = determine_resume_phase(last_logged)
   754→        if last_phase == "done":
   755→            accuracy = get_logged_accuracy(last_logged)
   756→            if accuracy is not None and accuracy >= ACCURACY_THRESHOLD:
   757→                print(f"Iteration {last_logged} already reached target accuracy ({accuracy:.1%}). Nothing to do.")
   758→                return
   759→            start_iteration = last_logged + 1
   760→        else:
   761→            start_iteration = last_logged
   762→    else:
   763→        start_iteration = 1
   764→
   765→    print("=" * 60)
   766→    print("SELF-IMPROVING TITLE EXTRACTION LOOP")
   767→    print(f"Max iterations: {MAX_ITERATIONS}")
   768→    print(f"Accuracy threshold: {ACCURACY_THRESHOLD:.0%}")
   769→    print(f"Input dir: {INPUT_DIR}")
   770→    if start_iteration > 1:
   771→        phase = determine_resume_phase(start_iteration)
   772→        print(f"Resuming: iteration {start_iteration}, phase '{phase}'")
   773→    print("=" * 60)
   774→    print()
   775→
   776→    # Verify CLI works before starting
   777→    print("Pre-flight: verifying CLI...")
   778→    if not verify_cli():
   779→        print("FATAL: CLI verification failed. Fix before running the loop.")
   780→        sys.exit(1)
   781→    print()
   782→
   783→    for iteration in range(start_iteration, MAX_ITERATIONS + 1):
   784→        phase = determine_resume_phase(iteration)
   785→
   786→        if phase == "done":
   787→            print(f"Iteration {iteration} already completed. Skipping.")
   788→            continue
   789→
   790→        print("=" * 60)
   791→        print(f"ITERATION {iteration}" + (f"  (resuming from '{phase}')" if phase != "evaluate" else ""))
   792→        print("=" * 60)
   793→
   794→        iter_dir = find_iter_dir(iteration)
   795→        all_results: list[dict] | None = None
   796→
   797→        # Ensure logs dir exists for this iteration
   798→        if iter_dir:
   799→            (iter_dir / "logs").mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   800→
   801→        # --- EVALUATE ---
   802→        if phase == "evaluate":
   803→            # Create provisional log dir (iter_dir doesn't exist yet)
   804→            commit_hash = get_commit_hash()
   805→            provisional_log_dir = DOCS_DIR / f"{iteration}-{commit_hash}" / "logs"
   806→            provisional_log_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   807→
   808→            try:
   809→                iter_dir, combined_accuracy, all_results = phase_evaluate(iteration, log_dir=provisional_log_dir)
   810→            except RuntimeError as e:
   811→                print(f"  FATAL: {e}. Stopping.")
   812→                break
   813→
   814→            if combined_accuracy >= ACCURACY_THRESHOLD:
   815→                print(f"\n{'=' * 60}")
   816→                print(f"TARGET ACCURACY REACHED: {combined_accuracy:.1%}")
   817→                print(f"Stopping after iteration {iteration}")
   818→                print(f"{'=' * 60}")
   819→                break
   820→
   821→        assert iter_dir is not None, f"No iteration directory found for iteration {iteration}"
   822→
   823→        # --- ANALYZE ---
   824→        if phase in ("evaluate", "analyze"):
   825→            print(f"\nAnalyzing failures with Claude (Sonnet)...")
   826→            if all_results is None:
   827→                all_results = load_results_from_file(iter_dir)
   828→            try:
   829→                phase_analyze(iter_dir, all_results)
   830→            except RuntimeError as e:
   831→                print(f"  FATAL: {e}. Stopping.")
   832→                break
   833→
   834→        # --- PLAN ---
   835→        if phase in ("evaluate", "analyze", "plan"):
   836→            print(f"\nPlanning improvements with Claude (Opus)...")
   837→            try:
   838→                phase_plan(iter_dir, iteration)
   839→            except RuntimeError as e:
   840→                print(f"  FATAL: {e}. Stopping.")
   841→                break
   842→
   843→        # --- EXECUTE + VERIFY + COMMIT ---
   844→        print(f"\nExecuting improvements with Claude (Sonnet)...")
   845→        try:
   846→            phase_execute(iter_dir)
   847→        except RuntimeError as e:
   848→            print(f"  FATAL: {e}. Stopping.")
   849→            break
   850→
   851→        # Commit changes
   852→        print(f"\nCommitting changes...")
   853→        try:
   854→            commit_hash = git_commit_all(f"title-extraction: iteration {iteration}")
   855→            print(f"  Committed: {commit_hash}")
   856→        except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:
   857→            # git commit exits 1 both for "nothing to commit" and real failures.
   858→            # Check git status to distinguish.
   859→            status = subprocess.run(
   860→                ["git", "status", "--porcelain"],
   861→                capture_output=True, text=True, cwd=str(PROJECT_ROOT),

   865→                commit_hash = get_commit_hash()
   866→            else:

   869→
   870→        # Mark iteration as committed so resume skips it
   871→        (iter_dir / "committed").write_text(f"{commit_hash}\n")
   872→
   873→        # Prepare next iteration directory
   874→        next_dir = DOCS_DIR / f"{iteration + 1}-{commit_hash}"
   875→        next_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   876→
   877→        print(f"\n  Next iteration dir: {next_dir.relative_to(PROJECT_ROOT)}")
   878→        print()
   879→
   880→    else:
   881→        print(f"\n{'=' * 60}")
   882→        print(f"MAX ITERATIONS ({MAX_ITERATIONS}) REACHED")
   883→        print(f"Final accuracy may not have met threshold.")
   884→        print(f"{'=' * 60}")
   885→
   886→    # Print summary

   888→    if ITER_LOG.exists():
